title: Fix missing mutex on createChannel/deleteChannel/createDecision
description:
**Found during QA deep audit of B8/B16 fixes.**

`createChannel()` (line ~665), `deleteChannel()` (line ~886), and `createDecision()` (line ~1026) perform read-check-write sequences without holding `this.mutex`.

**Race scenario for createChannel:**
1. Two concurrent calls with same channel name
2. Both pass the duplicate name check at line ~681
3. Both write the channel — duplicate created

**Fix:** Wrap these methods in `this.mutex.runExclusive()` like `pinMessage`/`unpinMessage` already are.

Also document the lock ordering constraint: `this.mutex` → `channelMutex` (never reverse). `claimNextTask` holds `this.mutex` and calls `sendMessage` which acquires `channelMutex` — this ordering must be preserved.
definitionOfDone: createChannel, deleteChannel, createDecision wrapped in mutex.runExclusive
Lock ordering documented in code comment
No concurrent createChannel can create duplicates

implementationPlan.description:
P0: Wrap createChannel (line 705), deleteChannel (line 933), and createDecision (line 1073) in this.mutex.runExclusive(). Each method performs read-check-write without holding the mutex. The duplicate name check in createChannel and the file operations in deleteChannel must be atomic. Note: createDecision calls sendMessage (acquires channelMutex) — safe because lock order is mutex then channelMutex. Add explicit error handling in each wrapper to log and re-throw.

Validate: npx tsc --noEmit
implementationPlan.note: Wrapped all three methods in this.mutex.runExclusive(). createDecision includes lock ordering comment since it calls sendMessage (acquires channelMutex).

implementationPlan.description:
P1: Add lock ordering comment near mutex declarations in StateManager.ts documenting: this.mutex must be acquired before channelMutex. Methods like claimNextTask hold mutex and call sendMessage which acquires channelMutex — never reverse. This prevents deadlocks.

Validate: npx tsc --noEmit

P1: Add concurrency test for createChannel. Two concurrent createChannel calls with same name via Promise.allSettled — exactly one should succeed, the other should throw 'already exists'. Test verifies no duplicate channels created and error handling works correctly.

Validate: npm run test
implementationPlan.note: Added concurrency test to existing StateManager.test.ts. Test fires two concurrent createChannel with same name, verifies exactly one succeeds and one throws 'already exists'.

implementationPlan.description: P2: Run full validation: npx tsc --noEmit && npm run test && npm run build
